channel.title: The NHS Alliance - News
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for The NHS Alliance news

item.title: The NHS Alliance responds to the Nuffield Trust’s report on women’s experiences after birth
item.description: Longstanding racial and mental health inequalities affecting women remain, something which is deeply concerning, especially as these disparities continue into motherhood.

item.title: Financial pressures reinforce need for service transformation
item.description: The Welsh NHS Confederation responds to Audit Wales' report on NHS Wales organisations' 2025-26 accounts.

item.title: NHS leaders welcome Welsh Government’s ambitions for prevention agenda
item.description: The Welsh NHS Confederation responds to the Deputy Minister for Public Health and Preventative Health's statement on the Welsh Government's cross-government commitments to prevention.

item.title: NHS leaders working hard as extreme heat strains frontline services
item.description: David Williams responds to latest performance stats and says NHS leaders have been working hard to respond to the growing pressures created by these heatwaves.

item.title: From fast food to flood risk: how planning decisions affect our health and wellbeing
item.description: The Welsh NHS Confederation and Public Health Wales publish an explainer about local planning systems' impact on the population's health and wellbeing.

item.title: The NHS Alliance responds to rollout of new AI tools across the NHS
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane said it will play a key role in plans to transform the NHS and improve productivity.

item.title: The NHS Alliance responds as consultant doctors vote in favour of strike mandate
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane says health leaders will be disappointed that senior doctors have voted in favour of potential industrial action.

item.title: Whole-system approach needed to ensure system resilience
item.description: Director of the Welsh NHS Confederation, Darren Hughes, responds to the Cabinet Minister’s statement on health and care winter preparedness 2026/27.

item.title: The NHS Alliance responds to Baroness Amos' maternity report
item.description: Matthew Hopkins says that NHS leaders stand ready to play their part in improving maternity services for women, babies, families and staff

item.title: The NHS Alliance responds to BMA resident doctor ballot
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane responds to BMA resident doctor ballot

item.title: Strengthening leadership and shaping policy: Welsh NHS Confederation publishes 2025/26 impact
item.description: The Welsh NHS Confederation outlines its impact on behalf of Welsh NHS leaders in 2025/26.

item.title: Lessons identified in Ockenden review must lead to real change
item.description: Matthew Hopkins responds to the Ockenden review and the government's decision to roll out Martha's Rule to all maternity services.

item.title: A strong social care sector is crucial for the NHS
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane responds to the Skills for care report on adult social care.

item.title: Joint statement from NI Health and Social Care (HSC) system leaders on the Urology Services Inquiry
item.description: Health and social care leaders in NI comment on the findings of the Urology Services Inquiry

item.title: NHS leaders in Wales welcome the focus on prevention in the Supplementary Budget
item.description: The Welsh NHS Confederation responded to the statement by the Cabinet Minister for Finance on the Welsh Government’s Supplementary Budget for 2026 to 2027.

item.title: Hot weather adds to NHS pressures
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane responds to the red heat-health alerts for six regions in England issued by the UK Health Security Agency.

item.title: Additional funding for NHS Wales welcomed, but whole-system approach needed
item.description: The Welsh NHS Confederation responds to the Welsh Government’s announcement of an extra £145 million for the NHS in Wales as part of Supplementary Budget for 2026 to 2027.

item.title: Joint statement from NI Health and Social Care (HSC) system leaders on Muckamore inquiry findings
item.description: Health and social care leaders in Northern Ireland issue statement in response to the findings of the Muckamore Abbey Hospital Inquiry

item.title: Industrial action will cause "hugely frustrating" delays for patients
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane responds to a warning by NHS England that hot weather and the World Cup could add to pressures posed by resident doctors' industrial action in the coming week.

item.title: Suspension of strikes is a vital chance to reset the conversation
item.description: Sir Ciarán Devane comments on the BMA's decision to suspend the upcoming strikes.
